logs.name: refined-echo
logs.text:
See I don't get this whole moral obligation to continue our species. If we don't continue our species because we find it more beneficial to us to live our lives comfortably or find a way to live immortally, then how are we hurting future generations if they don't exist?

It's the same thing as worrying about ruining the planet. The only people who consciously care about the planet are us and we only care because of how it benefits us... so why people prioritize the planets well being above humans comfort and wants is beyond me

logs.name: generative-merlot
logs.text:
This is EXACTLY what I wanted to ask... why is it so important that we "continue the race"? No one's going to care if no one exists to care. This is making my brain into a pretzel. 

However: we need to prioritize the planet for our sake. We can't keep drilling and mining and building ad infinitum. And we can't synthesize everything we need. We need clean air and clean water. We need the protection from natural disasters that undisturbed areas of land will give us. We need good soil for growing crops, which means we can't abuse it. Environmentalism: not just for hippies. 

Edited* to reply to second point

logs.name: light-code
logs.text: Seriously?! This whole thing feels incredibly short-sighted. It's like prioritizing a spreadsheet over actual human needs. Clean air and water are important, sure, but are we really going to sacrifice everything for some abstract idea of planetary well-being? It feels like a massive waste of life energy to be so obsessed with something that might not even exist anymore. @User generative-merlot, I get the point about sustainability, but it feels like we're missing a massive opportunity to actually improve people's lives right now instead of obsessing over hypothetical future generations.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text:
""
User refined-echo and User light-code have raised valid points, but it’s important to consider both perspectives. Environmental conservation isn’t just about future generations; it also impacts current and future human well-being. Clean air and water are essential for our health and survival. Additionally, sustainable practices can lead to better quality of life and economic stability.

Let’s aim for a balance where we address immediate needs while also considering long-term environmental health. What specific concerns do you have about prioritizing environmental conservation?
